title: "Cerulean Scepter Puzzle v10847"

description: |
  At 21:10 the Cerulean Scepter vanished from a sealed pedestal.
  Four badge-holders (1  2  3  4) were seen nearby.
  Exactly one suspect lies in every statement they utter; the other three speak only truths.
  Exactly one suspect is guilty.

  Suspect 1:
    (1) 'If $L2$ then $\lnot G3$.'
    (2) 'If $T3$ then $\lnot P4$.'
    (3) '$P2 \oplus L4$.'
    (4) '$G3 \to L4$.'

  Suspect 2:
    (1) '$P1$.'
    (2) '$L4$.'
    (3) '$G1 \oplus L4$.'
    (4) 'If $T1$ then $P3$.'

  Suspect 3:
    (1) '$\lnot P3$.'
    (2) 'If $T4$ then $P2$.'
    (3) '$L2 \land \lnot P1$.'
    (4) 'If $G2$ then $\lnot L1$.'

  Suspect 4:
    (1) 'If $L2$ then $\lnot P3$.'
    (2) 'If $L1$ then $P1$.'
    (3) '$\lnot G4$.'
    (4) '$L2 \lor G1$.'

  Notation:
    $Lx$: x lies in all statements. $Tx$: x is entirely truthful.
    $Gx$: x is guilty. $Px$: x was present at 21:10. $\oplus$: exclusive-or.

question: "Who stole the Cerulean Scepter? Return a single digit (1–4)."

answer: >
  explanation: |
    Assume each suspect is the sole liar and propagate consequences.

    • Case $L1$: 1(1) being false forces $L3$ true and $\lnot G3$ false ⇒ two liars – impossible.  
    • Case $L2$: All four statements of 2 are false ⇒  
      – 2(1) false ⇒ $\lnot P1$.  
      – 2(2) false ⇒ $L4$ false ⇒ $T4$ true.  
      – 2(3) false ⇒ $G1$ false.  
      – 2(4) false with $T1$ true forces $\lnot P3$.  
      Truthful 1, 3, 4 now give  
      • 1(1) true ⇒ $L2\to\lnot G3$ ⇒ $\lnot G3$.  
      • 1(2) true with $T3$ true ⇒ $\lnot P4$.  
      • 3(2) true ($T4$ → $P2$) ⇒ $P2$.  
      • 3(4) true ($G2$ → $\lnot L1$) demands $G2$.  
      All conditions coexist with a single liar ($L2$) and a single thief ($G2$).  

    • Case $L3$: 3(1)–(4) all false yields $\lnot P3$ false ⇒ $P3$, but 4(1) true forces $\lnot P3$ – contradiction.  
    • Case $L4$: 4(3) false ⇒ $G4$, while 1(2) true needs $\lnot P4$, conflicting with the thief’s presence – contradiction.

    Only the $L2$ & $G2$ scenario remains consistent.

  final: '2'
